criteria.label: Explain t-SNE's goal, high-D Gaussian affinities, low-D Student-t affinities, and KL objective in plain language.
criteria.example: In 3–4 sentences, describe why t-SNE maps neighbors close in high-D to nearby points in 2D and why Student-t tails help crowding.

criteria.label: Define perplexity and describe how it affects the t-SNE map.
criteria.example: Compare sklearn TSNE plots on the same scaled Iris data with perplexity=5 vs 30 and state which reveals smoother species groupings.

criteria.label: Run t-SNE with sklearn including scaling and optional PCA preprocessing.
criteria.example: On Digits, StandardScaler → PCA(50) → TSNE(2, perplexity=30, random_state=0); print Y.shape and list hyperparameters used.

criteria.label: Contrast t-SNE with PCA for visualization without re-deriving spectral theory.
criteria.example: On a curved 2D manifold embedded in 3D, note that PCA may overlap classes on PC1–PC2 while t-SNE separates local sheets—link to PCA topic for the linear baseline.

criteria.label: Identify common misinterpretations (global distance, feature use, transform of new points) and state correct practice.
criteria.example: Write one paragraph explaining why `clf.fit(tsne.fit_transform(X), y)` is wrong and what to do instead for modeling vs EDA.
